worthClicking.author: @minchoi
worthClicking.why: Anthropic killed an entire company's Claude access overnight with no explanation and a Google Form appeal — a concrete trust and reliability failure that matters for anyone building on the API.

worthClicking.author: @RoundtableSpace
worthClicking.why: Chatbot Arena data showing Opus 4.6 beats 4.7 on longer queries and domain-specific tasks directly contradicts Anthropic's upgrade narrative and is worth tracking.

worthClicking.author: @levie
worthClicking.why: Aaron Levie's argument that headless enterprise platforms create more opportunity than they destroy is the clearest articulation of the agent-native SaaS thesis from someone operating at scale.

worthClicking.author: @gregisenberg
worthClicking.why: Lays out a specific mechanic for how agent-native startups undercut legacy SaaS — useful framing for product strategy, not just hype.

worthClicking.author: @ClaudeDevs
worthClicking.why: Claude Code v2.1.113 ships a native binary instead of JS, dropping the Node.js runtime dependency — a practical change for anyone running it in CI or toolchains.

worthClicking.author: @RobertMSterling
worthClicking.why: Cynical but pointed take that Dario's job-displacement rhetoric is IPO positioning to expand TAM to 'all white-collar labor' — worth reading for the counterargument.

worthClicking.author: @thsottiaux
worthClicking.why: Codex got a major capability update (computer use, browser, 90+ plugins, multi-terminal, SSH) — a direct competitive signal against Claude Code.

worthClicking.author: @jpschroeder
worthClicking.why: Condensed version of the Opus 4.7 skeptic case — buggy Claude Code, constant API downtime, unfinished Claude Desktop — worth knowing because it's circulating widely.

voices.why: Thinking clearly about the agent-native enterprise shift from an operator's perspective, not a hype perspective.
Official Anthropic dev account posting actionable Claude Code release notes.
Surfacing real vendor reliability failures with specifics, useful for AI product risk assessment.
Posting actual Arena leaderboard data on model comparisons rather than just vibes.

examples: Strait of Hormuz open/closed play-by-play (repeated, market-noise framing)
Viral meme/joke posts with no operator relevance (tattoos, Subaru divorces, dick jokes)
